summary: Fixed the Pokemon Stadium stage in nonmatching builds by correcting data definitions and text/SIS handling in grstadium.c, and by replacing the standalone grPs_804D6978 sbss symbol with the existing stage yakumono parameter object yaku. The PR states that Pokemon Stadium can now load and appears functional in nonmatching builds, including transformations, text display, and jumbotron player/stage camera views.
what_changed: Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt so .sbss:0x804D6978 is named yaku as a local 0x4 object instead of grPs_804D6978 as a global 0x8 object.
Expanded an anonymous static struct near the top of grstadium.c from two ints plus padding into explicit integer fields x8, xC, x10, x14, and x18 before the color fields.
Replaced extern declarations for grPs_803B7F68 and grPs_803B7F74 with static const Vec3 zero vectors.
Added a static TextGlyphTexture grPs_8049F040 and wired it into HSD_SisLib_804D1124[1][2].textures in grStadium_801D3460.
Adjusted player list collection in grStadium_801D3460 to store gm_80160A60 results at sp38[var_r28] only after using a temporary, avoiding the previous sp38[i] indexing behavior.
Changed a condition in grStadium_801D3460 from !temp_r3_4->x10 to an explicit temp_r3_4->x10 == 0 while preserving the bitwise OR with !temp_r3_4->x0_6.
Defined previously externed float constants grPs_804DAF2C through grPs_804DAF74 in grstadium.c.
Corrected grPs_803B7F8C U8Vec4 data layout by moving values from the first byte to the fourth byte: {0,0,0,0x50}, {0,0,0,0x40}, {0,0,0,0x30}, {0,0,0,0x20}.
Removed the local grPs_804D6978 pointer struct declaration and updated grStadium_801D4548 to access yakumono timing/range parameters through yaku->x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, and x18.
smart_moves: Recognized that grPs_804D6978 was not a separate Pokemon Stadium global but part of the stage yakumono parameter object, reducing duplicate or incorrect sbss modeling.
Turned missing extern data into local static const definitions where the diff showed simple zero vectors and scalar float constants, making the nonmatching build self-contained.
Fixed SIS text texture hookup by using the actual nested HSD_SisLib_804D1124[1][2].textures field instead of leaving the assignment commented behind a type-system TODO.
Preserved a potential codegen-sensitive boolean expression by using an explicit comparison with bitwise OR rather than rewriting it into a short-circuit logical expression.
Corrected byte ordering for grPs_803B7F8C as U8Vec4 data, which likely matters for display color/alpha behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching build crashes or fails to load a stage, verify that sbss symbols are not artificial splits of a larger stage parameter object. Here grPs_804D6978 was merged into yaku.
Extern declarations for data are not enough for nonmatching builds if the object is expected to be emitted by C; define simple constants locally when evidence supports the values.
For UI/SIS work, prefer fixing the struct access path once the type is known rather than leaving commented-out assignments. The working access here was HSD_SisLib_804D1124[1][2].textures.
Array compaction logic should match the intended runtime structure, not just compile: this PR changed player camera data collection from indexing by player slot to indexing by active-player count.
U8Vec4 literals are byte-order sensitive. Check whether a value belongs in r/g/b/a rather than assuming the first initializer position is correct.
Stage yakumono parameters can contain timing and range fields used across transformation logic, so reusing the shared yaku object can fix both symbol correctness and runtime behavior.
naming_conventions: Pokemon Stadium stage symbols use the grPs_ prefix for file-local inferred data such as grPs_803B7F68, grPs_803B7F74, grPs_803B7F8C, and grPs_804DAF2C.
The yakumono parameter object is named yaku in symbols.txt and is local at .sbss:0x804D6978.
The stage function names retain address-style decomp names such as grStadium_801D3460 and grStadium_801D4548.
Anonymous or partially known structs continue to use offset-style field names such as x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, and x18 when semantic names are not yet proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit zero-valued static const Vec3 definitions to replace externs when the diff identifies the data as {0, 0, 0}.
Keep bitwise OR in conditions when matching or behavior may depend on both sides being evaluated; this PR used `(temp_r3_4->x10 == 0) | !temp_r3_4->x0_6`.
Model data layout by exact byte positions for U8Vec4 constants; the PR moved 0x50/0x40/0x30/0x20 to the fourth component.
Merge symbol-table entries into the correct aggregate object when the prior size/scope appears wrong; grPs_804D6978 became local yaku with size 0x4 in symbols.txt.
Define float constants near the code that uses them when resolving nonmatching-link/runtime failures from previously externed constants.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body explicitly listed the intended fixes: merge grPs_804D6978 into stage yakumono parameters, define previously externed floats/vectors, fix grPs_803B7F8C data, and fix text handling in grStadium_801D3460.
follow_up_queries: Search for other stage .sbss symbols that may actually be yaku or aggregate yakumono parameter fields.
Audit other commented HSD_SisLib_804D1124 texture assignments now that the nested SIS type path is known.
Check whether TextGlyphTexture size is correctly modeled, because the PR added a TODO noting the object is only 0x100 while TextGlyphTexture is 0x200.
Look for other U8Vec4 data arrays where alpha values may have been placed in the wrong component.
Verify whether the anonymous stadium parameter struct can receive semantic field names for x0 through x18 after more yakumono uses are understood.
